reasoning: Historical base rates for armed militant groups fighting existential conflicts voluntarily agreeing to fully disarm and formally announcing it are extremely low (< 2%). In the case of Hamas, as of the knowledge cutoff date (November 9, 2025), there is intense pressure from the US and Arab nations via a 20-point peace plan to force disarmament. While US Envoy Steve Witkoff claimed informally on Nov 6 that Hamas told negotiators they "would disarm," Hamas has explicitly omitted this from their official public communications. On October 3, Hamas stated disarmament should be discussed under a future "comprehensive Palestinian national framework" (contingent on future conditions). Most recently, on November 9, the Al-Qassam Brigades explicitly denied any intention to surrender. Because the resolution criteria require an *official, formal policy directive announcement* of disarmament, and explicitly rule out hearsay, informal statements, and plans contingent on future conditions, the likelihood of Hamas clearing this incredibly high bar publicly by December 31, 2025, is practically nil.
